config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar negara atau mayoritas tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat masyarakat, ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu luas, yang sering dirugikan justru kelompok kecil atau orang biasa yang suaranya lemah. Bagi saya, prioritas yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have a long history of seeing majorities abuse that power when individual rights are treated as negotiable. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be limited by constitutional rights, or else “the greater good” becomes an excuse for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I also think the phrase is too broad: sometimes society’s interests are legitimate, but sometimes they are just the preferences of the loudest group. The better principle is that individual rights should be the default priority, with narrow exceptions when there is a clear, compelling harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan yang paling mudah dikorbankan biasanya justru orang kecil, minoritas, atau mereka yang tidak punya akses kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan dan ketertiban, tetapi tanpa perlindungan hak individu, mayoritas bisa seenaknya membungkam atau merugikan yang lemah. Jadi menurut saya, hak individu harus jadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang mudah disingkirkan oleh klaim kepentingan সমাজ.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to justify abuses by governments and majorities. Public safety and shared needs are real, but they should be limited by strong rights protections, or else “society” becomes a vague excuse for censorship, discrimination, or forcing minorities to absorb the costs. The strongest version of the other side is that some collective problems require collective action, and I do not deny that, but that is not the same as saying the group should outrank the person by default. For me, the better standard is individual rights first, with narrow exceptions only when there is a clear and serious harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia frasa “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang paling merugikan orang kecil. Memang ada urusan kolektif seperti keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengalahkan hak individu. Justru karena masyarakat kita majemuk, hak individu perlu dijadikan pagar supaya mayoritas tidak seenaknya menekan minoritas atau kelompok yang lemah. Jadi bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh diposisikan di atas hak individu secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a default rule. From a U.S. perspective, that idea has too often been used to excuse government overreach and majority pressure, especially against unpopular minorities, and I am not willing to hand over that much power to a vague notion of “the public good.” Collective needs like safety, health, and infrastructure matter, but they only justify limits when the harm is clear, serious, and narrowly defined. In my view, a free society protects the person first and asks society to prove why an exception is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
